novel_features.name: What Did I Miss
novel_features.description: See every tech headline from the last N hours — the perfect catch-up when you've been away
novel_features.rationale: Requires time-indexed local cache of river headlines that the website doesn't offer as a single filtered view
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to brief a user on what happened in tech while they were in meetings, this is the single command that answers it

novel_features.name: Topic Tracker
novel_features.description: Save topics and get alerts when they hit Techmeme — persistent monitoring without browser tabs
novel_features.rationale: Requires local topic registry and match-on-sync logic that doesn't exist in any Techmeme interface
novel_features.example: techmeme-pp-cli track add 'OpenAI' && techmeme-pp-cli track check --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents monitoring specific companies or technologies can subscribe to exactly what matters without polling the full feed

novel_features.name: Source Rankings
novel_features.description: See which publications dominate Techmeme and track source frequency over time
novel_features.rationale: Combines OPML leaderboard data with locally cached headline-source attribution for frequency analysis
novel_features.why_it_matters: When analyzing media landscape or choosing which publications to prioritize, this gives hard data on source influence

novel_features.name: Trending Topics
novel_features.description: Extract the hottest topics from recent headlines using frequency analysis on cached data
novel_features.rationale: Requires NLP-lite term extraction across many headlines — only possible with local data
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to answer 'what's hot in tech right now' with data instead of vibes

novel_features.name: Daily Digest
novel_features.description: Get a day's tech news grouped by topic — the briefing you'd write if you had time
novel_features.rationale: Requires grouping headlines by topic similarity from the local cache, which no single page provides
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to produce a tech news briefing for a specific date, this structures raw headlines into a readable summary

novel_features.name: Story Velocity
novel_features.description: Find stories that are blowing up — multiple sources covering the same topic in a short window
novel_features.rationale: Requires time-series analysis of headline clusters from cached data — the website shows importance but not acceleration
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to identify breaking news vs steady coverage, velocity shows what's accelerating now

novel_features.name: Author Spotlight
novel_features.description: Find all Techmeme headlines by a specific journalist across the cached archive
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-source author index built from parsed river data — the website has no author search
novel_features.why_it_matters: When tracking a specific journalist's coverage or building a media contact list, this surfaces their Techmeme footprint

narrative.headline: Every Techmeme headline, searchable and cached locally — plus topic tracking, trending analysis, and catch-up workflows no other tool has.
narrative.value_prop: The Techmeme CLI puts the tech industry's most trusted news curation into your terminal. Sync headlines to a local SQLite store, then search, filter by time, track topics, and analyze which stories and sources are dominating. The 'since' command answers the question every tech professional asks: 'what did I miss?'

quickstart.comment: Sync 5 days of headlines to local SQLite
What happened in the last 4 hours?
What topics are hot right now?

troubleshoots.fix: Run 'techmeme-pp-cli sync' first to populate the local cache from the 5-day river archive
The RSS feed only carries 15 items. Use 'river' or 'sync' for the full 5-day archive (150+ headlines)

troubleshoots.symptom: Search finds nothing for a recent topic
troubleshoots.fix: The search endpoint indexes Techmeme headlines only — topics that haven't made Techmeme's curated feed won't appear

narrative.when_to_use: Use the Techmeme CLI when you need curated, authoritative tech news without opening a browser. Ideal for morning catch-up ('since 8h'), topic monitoring ('track add OpenAI'), source analysis ('sources'), and AI agents that need to answer 'what's happening in tech right now' with structured data.

recipes.title: Morning briefing
recipes.explanation: Catch up on overnight tech news with just the essentials — title, source, and timestamp

recipes.explanation: Find all recent Techmeme headlines about Apple

recipes.explanation: Find stories gaining momentum — multiple sources in a short window

recipes.explanation: Today's tech news grouped by topic for a quick read
